title: 'Can China, US transcend Thucydides Trap?' Xi's poser to Trump reveals big stakes
content: Chinese President Xi Jinping invoked the "Thucydides Trap" during talks with US President Donald Trump, highlighting concerns that the rising power's growth could lead to conflict with the established dominant power. This theory, popularized by Graham Allison, suggests structural tensions make war more likely, a concept now central to global geopolitical debates involving trade wars and technological competition.

title: 'Unilateral coercive steps, sanctions': Jaishankar takes veiled dig at US
content: External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar criticized "unilateral coercive measures" and sanctions, stating they disproportionately harm developing nations and cannot replace diplomacy. He also voiced concern over the West Asia crisis, emphasizing the need for uninterrupted maritime traffic and a sustained ceasefire in Gaza, advocating for a two-state solution.

title: 'Why this show off of independence?’: SC flags CEC appointment mechanism
content: The Supreme Court questioned the current selection process for Election Commissioners. It raised concerns about a cabinet minister being part of the panel. The court noted this could lead to a 2:1 majority favoring the Prime Minister. It suggested an independent process is crucial for upholding democracy and public confidence in the Election Commission's impartiality.

title: Why ChatGPT devs who became overnight millionaires need to thank Mark Zuckerberg
content: OpenAI has enabled over 600 employees to sell shares worth $6.6 billion, with some cashing out up to $30 million. This significant wealth creation is fueled by the intense AI talent war, particularly after Meta's substantial compensation offers, prompting OpenAI to boost salaries and equity packages to retain its workforce.

title: Watch: UP man swept into air while holding tin shed during violent storm, survives fall
content: A terrifying incident in Bareilly, Uttar Pradesh, saw a youth named Nanhe lifted several feet into the air along with a massive tin shed during a powerful dust storm. Villagers witnessed the shed being uprooted and Nanhe clinging to it before being thrown into a maize field. He is currently in critical condition with multiple fractures and severe injuries.

title: India squads for Afghanistan Test, ODIs likely to be picked on May 19
content: Indian selectors are poised to finalise squads for the Afghanistan Test and subsequent ODIs on May 19. Shubman Gill is expected to lead a strong batting lineup for the Test. With Jasprit Bumrah likely to be rested for the contest that doesn't form part of the World Test Championship, plenty of fresh faces are expected to make the cut. Regarding the ODI selection, no major surprises are expected

title: Gurgaon student held for NEET paper leak is on school banner of top achievers
content: A studious farmer's son, Yash Yadav, hailed as an achiever by his local school, has been arrested by Rajasthan police in connection with the NEET-UG paper leak.  Investigators suspect he received and sold a 'guess paper' for lakhs, potentially implicating him in a wider racket that has disrupted the medical entrance exam for lakhs of aspirants.

title: Why Trump aides left their phones behind before entering China
content: During President Trump's visit to China, American officials and CEOs implemented a 'digital lockdown,' using 'clean' devices and leaving personal electronics behind. This precaution stems from a long-standing US government assumption that all devices and networks in China are potentially compromised and subject to surveillance.

title: Tamil Nadu govt employees get 2% DA hike as CM Vijay raises allowance to 60%
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay has announced a 2% dearness allowance hike for state government employees and teachers, raising it from 58% to 60% effective January 1. This decision, benefiting approximately 16 lakh individuals, aligns with the Union government's revision and will incur an additional annual expenditure of ₹1,230 crore for the state exchequer.

title: West Bengal makes ‘Vande Mataram’ must in school morning assemblies
content: West Bengal schools must now sing 'Vande Mataram' daily during morning assembly. This directive follows a Union Home Ministry notice. School heads face confusion regarding the mandatory singing of the state song 'Banglar mati Banglar jal' alongside 'Vande Mataram' and the National Anthem. Some schools have already begun implementing the order, while others await clarification before the summer break concludes.

title: Watch: Lawyer Mamata dons black gown, heckled outside Calcutta HC
content: Former Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee appeared as counsel at the Calcutta High Court, presenting evidence of post-poll violence. She alleged that children and women were not spared and submitted a list of alleged murder victims, urging the court to protect the people of Bengal. The Trinamool Congress lauded her commitment to truth and justice.

title: Meta's highest-paid employee is not happy with 'just money motivated' tag for his team
content: Meta SuperIntelligence Lab chief Alexandr Wang is pushing back on the idea that his team joined for the money. On the Core Memory podcast, Wang said the real draw was compute access, talent density, and research freedom—not the $100 million packages Meta reportedly offered. He also addressed the viral soup story involving Mark Zuckerberg and an OpenAI recruit.

title: Why is stock market up? Sensex jumps 1,000 points; Nifty50 above 23,700 - top reasons
content: Despite the bullish undertone in equities, several risk factors continue to keep investors on edge. The rupee touched a fresh record low after breaching the 95.8 mark against the US dollar for the first time.

title: Green card, citizenship application processing continues to face delays
content: New USCIS fingerprint checks are causing significant delays for immigration applications, including permanent residency and citizenship. Officials must resubmit biometrics for pending cases to the FBI's upgraded system, even if previously cleared. Attorneys warn this could lead to increased scrutiny of minor past interactions and disproportionately impact immigrants from certain regions.

title: 'Question paper circulated through couriers': Jaipur's Biwal family at centre of NEET paper leak
content: The CBI has arrested five and detained four in the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak, uncovering a multi-state distribution network originating in Pune. A Rajasthan family, the Biwals, is central to the probe, with investigators reconstructing the leak's path through couriers and digital channels. The investigation was triggered by a whistleblower's alert.

title: ‘They hijacked the body’: RG Kar parents move court, demand arrest of ex-MLA Nirmal Ghosh
content: The mother of a rape-murder victim, now an MLA, has moved court seeking the arrest of three Trinamool Congress leaders, alleging they attempted to destroy evidence and hastily cremated her daughter's body. The court has fixed June 5 for the next hearing, with the CBI stating they will submit a response regarding the arrests.

title: Turmeric to serums: Night-time skincare essentials for repair, glow and anti-ageing
content: Your skin undergoes its most powerful transformation at night, actively repairing damage from daily aggressors. Understanding your skin's needs at different ages enhances your night routine, focusing on repair, hydration, glow and anti-aging. Consistent, intentional night-time rituals support your skin's natural ability to rebuild and renew, leading to healthier, glowing skin.

title: ‘Unacceptable’: MEA condemns attack on Indian-flagged ship off Oman coast
content: India has strongly condemned an attack on an Indian-flagged ship near Oman, deeming it 'unacceptable' and expressing deep concern over ongoing threats to commercial shipping. All Indian crew members aboard the vessel are reported safe, with thanks extended to Omani authorities for their rescue.

title: 'Sports need you': Kohli told 'play as long as possible' by IPL great - Watch
content: Dwayne Bravo praised Virat Kohli after his unbeaten 105 guided Royal Challengers Bengaluru to a six-wicket win over Kolkata Knight Riders in IPL 2026. In a viral dressing-room video, Bravo told Kohli, “Play as long as possible, the sport needs you.” Kohli’s knock helped RCB chase 193 and move to the top of the points table.

title: 'Bengal not bulldozer state': Mamata dons lawyer’s gown, argues post-poll violence case in HC
content: Former West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee appeared before the Calcutta High Court, urging protection for citizens amidst allegations of post-poll violence. She accused police of inaction and highlighted incidents of attacks, intimidation, and threats, seeking urgent judicial intervention to ensure people's safety.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO day 57: Ranveer Singh film eyes Rs 1,150 crore
content: Aditya Dhar’s 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' continues its historic theatrical run, reaching a domestic net of Rs. 1,144.30 crore by Day 57. Currently the highest-grossing Hindi film ever, the Ranveer Singh starrer is now targeting the Rs. 1,150 crore milestone while eyeing Pushpa 2’s domestic record of Rs. 1,234.10 crore. With a worldwide gross of Rs. 1,796.04 crore, the espionage thriller maintains steady momentum ahead of its highly anticipated OTT release.

title: India’s wholesale inflation jumps to 8.3% in April as fuel, crude oil prices surge
content: India's wholesale inflation surged to 8.30% in April 2026, a significant jump from March's 3.88%. This spike is primarily attributed to soaring fuel, power, and crude oil prices, exacerbated by the West Asia crisis. Mineral oils, basic metals, and manufactured products also contributed to the rise, impacting various sectors.

title: Arresting rupee’s fall: India eyes cut in taxes on bond investments by foreigners
content: The proposal was suggested by the Reserve Bank of India and is currently under active consideration by the Finance Ministry, sources said. Talks around reducing taxes for overseas investors have accelerated amid efforts to slow the rupee’s decline, they added.

title: Hantavirus outbreak: Scientists ran out of funds while developing a vaccine, report says
content: A Hantavirus outbreak linked to the MV Hondius cruise ship has tragically claimed three lives this year, with 11 cases reported. While infections are rare, this outbreak highlights the urgent need for a vaccine, with research stalled by funding issues. International efforts are underway to manage the current situation, focusing on containment and monitoring.

title: VD Satheesan named next Kerala chief minister as Congress ends suspense
content: After days of intense consultations, the Congress has chosen VD Satheesan as the next chief minister of Kerala. The announcement follows the Congress-led UDF's decisive victory in the state elections, ending the Left Democratic Front's second consecutive term. Satheesan emerged as the preferred candidate over KC Venugopal and Ramesh Chennithala.

title: 'Not done yet': Gavaskar adds Virat Kohli twist to 'Gen Next' debate
content: Sunil Gavaskar lauded Virat Kohli's spectacular century, proving the enduring class of experienced players.  Kohli's masterclass in chasing, his ninth IPL hundred, and becoming the fastest to 14,000 T20 runs silenced doubters.  His innings highlighted his unparalleled ability to construct a chase, a feat few can match, reaffirming his legendary status.

title: History-maker at 12, how Faustino Oro became 'Messi of Chess'
content: Faustino Oro, a 12-year-old Argentine chess prodigy known as the “Messi of Chess,” has become the second-youngest Grandmaster in history. TimesofIndia.com follows his journey from learning chess during the COVID lockdown in Buenos Aires to moving to Barcelona and competing globally. Despite tougher FIDE rules, Oro’s talent, discipline, and love for the game helped him succeed.

title: Demo supercar deal turns into FIR battle! Karnataka HC calls out RTO
content: A Bengaluru man's supercar purchase led to a legal storm involving forgery claims and a police FIR. The Karnataka High Court, however, intervened, quashing the FIR and strongly criticizing an RTO officer's unauthorized vehicle seizure from the resident's home. The court emphasized the need for proper legal authority in such actions.

title: Fadnavis reaches Vidhan Bhavan on bike amid PM Modi's fuel-saving push
content: Maharashtra CM Devendra Fadnavis rode a motorcycle to the Vidhan Bhavan, mirroring PM Modi's call to conserve fuel amid West Asian energy concerns. Minister Nitesh Rane also walked to the Mantralaya. These actions follow the announcement of austerity measures, including halving ministerial convoy vehicles and cancelling foreign trips, to save fuel and foreign exchange.

title: Will Hardik Pandya play today against Punjab Kings in Dharamsala?
content: Mumbai Indians face Punjab Kings in Dharamsala, with captain Hardik Pandya highly unlikely to play due to back spasms. Suryakumar Yadav's availability is also uncertain as he remains in Mumbai after the birth of his child. Despite being eliminated, MI can impact Punjab's playoff hopes.

title: Significant decline! How much have India's forex reserves fallen since US-Iran war?
content: The erosion in reserves reflects not only intervention by policymakers to support domestic currencies, but also valuation losses in non-dollar assets, according to data compiled by Bloomberg.

title: Hormuz crisis: India eyes Rs 40k crore subsea pipeline for uninterrupted gas supplies
content: The proposed Middle East-India Deep-water Pipeline (MEIDP) is planned as a 2,000-kilometre underwater network running beneath the Arabian Sea and connecting Oman directly with the Gujarat coastline.

title: Why rising temperatures are triggering more snakebite incidents across India
content: As summer heat intensifies, India faces a surge in deadly snakebite incidents, with states like Karnataka and Kerala reporting significant increases. Experts attribute this rise to snakes seeking cooler temperatures and escaping monsoon floods, leading to more human encounters. Delayed treatment and reliance on myths tragically cost lives, underscoring the urgent need for immediate medical attention and preventative measures.

title: Who is VD Satheesan? Kerala’s new CM & grassroots leader who led UDF’s comeback
content: VD Satheesan has been appointed the new Kerala chief minister. The Congress party announced his selection after a period of deliberation. Satheesan, a lawyer and social worker with a strong legislative background, previously served as the leader of the opposition, guiding the party's resurgence.

title: Ahmedabad: Woman staffer caught on CCTV pocketing gold jewellery worth Rs 1.7 crore
content: A jeweller in Ahmedabad was shocked to discover an employee allegedly stole gold ornaments worth Rs 1.7 crore. CCTV footage reportedly shows the sales and marketing staffer pocketing chains, rings, and mangalsutras before leaving the showroom. Police are now actively searching for the accused woman.

title: 'It’s going to finish one day': Virat Kohli makes honest reflection
content: Virat Kohli delivered a masterful performance, smashing his ninth IPL century to lead Royal Challengers Bengaluru to a successful chase of 193 against Kolkata Knight Riders. This historic innings saw Kohli become the fastest player to 14,000 T20 runs and the first Indian with 10 T20 centuries, solidifying his legacy as a supreme chaser.

title: NEET UG paper leak case: 17-year-old Goa teen ends life over cancelled exam
content: A 17-year-old student from Curtorim died by suicide after being distressed by the NEET cancellation due to a paper leak. He left a note expressing his disinterest in further competitive exams. His father discovered the body at their residence.

title: Red Fort blast case: NIA files 7,500-page chargesheet in terror plot, names 10 accused
content: A massive 7,500-page chargesheet has been filed by the NIA against 10 individuals linked to the deadly Red Fort car bomb blast in November 2025. The accused, associated with an Al-Qaeda offshoot, allegedly plotted to overthrow the Indian government and impose Sharia rule. Investigations revealed procurement of specialized equipment and manufacturing of explosives by radicalized medical professionals.

title: India no longer a country that suffers, will strike back if Pakistan provokes us again: Rajnath Singh
content: Addressing a public gathering at Merta town in Nagaur after unveiling a statue of Rajput ruler Rao Duda, Mr. Singh said Operation Sindoor had sent a strong message to Pakistan

title: Kerala’s Chief Minister-designate V.D. Satheesan holds out an olive branch to other contenders for the top post
content: He terms Chennithala, United Democratic Front’s (UDF) campaign committee chairperson, ‘his leader, close friend and beloved party compatriot.’ He also appeared to place on record Venugopal’s ‘immense contribution to the UDF’s historic win.’

title: MoS Arjun Ram Meghwal takes e-rickshaw ride in Rajasthan’s Bikaner to promote fuel conservation
content: MoS Arjun Ram Meghwal arrived at Bikaner railway station from Delhi and chose to avoid a convoy of vehicles, opting instead to travel by e-rickshaw

title: CBI produces five NEET paper leak accused before Delhi court
content: The five accused were produced before CBI Special Judge Ajay Gupta after they were taken into custody from different States over their alleged involvement in the case

title: ECI announces SIR schedule for Telangana; BLO’s house-to-house visits from June 25-July 24
content: Final electoral rolls will be published on October 1, 2026. The number of electors in Telangana as on May 12, 2026, was 3,39,20,705

title: Farooq Abdullah backs RSS leader Dattatreya Hosabale’s call for dialogue with Pakistan
content: Speaking to reporters in Srinagar, Farooq Abdullah, a three-time Chief Minister of Jammu and Kashmir, also welcomed former Army chief M.M. Naravane backing Dattatreya Hosable

title: Kerala politics LIVE: Congress CM-designate Satheesan praises fellow candidates Chennithala and Venugopal in UDF's 'historic win'
content: Congress leaders and newly elected MLAs extended their greetings to V.D. Satheesan soon after the AICC announced him as the CM of Kerala

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly: Edappadi Palaniswami claims AIADMK rebel MLAs promised Cabinet berths for support to TVK govt.
content: AIADMK MLAs backing Mr. Palaniswami strongly objected to the Speaker J.C.D. Prabhakar calling S.P. Velumani, who heads a rebel group in the AIADMK, to speak on the motion.

title: Unelectrified hamlet in Kadambur Hills gets solar lighting facilities
content: Residents recalled that the hamlet first received electricity in 1972, when three street lights were installed. However, a forest fire in 1974 destroyed the wooden electricity poles, plunging the village back into darkness

title: Tamil Nadu government announces 2% increase in DA for govt. employees, teachers
content: The decision, which comes into with retrospective effect from January 1, 2026, will cost the exchequer an additional ₹1,230 crore, an official release said

title: West Bengal Class 12 results declared; overall pass percentage 91.23%
content: As many as 64 candidates — including 56 boys and eight girls — featured in the top 10 list. Adrito Pal of Narendrapur Ramakrishna Mission Vidyalaya secured the first rank in the state by scoring 496 out of 500 marks.

title: V.D. Satheesan to transform himself from the fiery Opposition leader to Chief Minister
content: The past decades also saw the former KSU leader Satheesan growing in stature and political clout both within the organisation and outside

title: At least three killed, 15 injured as blast triggers blaze at firecracker unit in M.P.'s Dewas; probe ordered
content: State government will provide an assistance of ₹4 lakh to the kin of each of the deceased, and the injured will receive free treatment

title: Watch: Rajinikanth recalls hilarious incident when nobody recognised him
content: Superstar actor Rajinikanth recalled a time when he stayed at the Art of Living centre near Bengaluru where to his shock, nobody recognised him. Speaking about the incident to an audience with Sri Sri Ravishankar by his side, Rajinikanth had the audience in splits when he said the incident “crushed his ego”.

title: Paper leaks ‘Demonetised’ students’ future: Telangana CM Revanth Reddy
content: Revanth Reddy urges Union Government and National Testing Agency (NTA) to conduct a transparent, time-bound and credible investigation into the matter

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly: DMK boycotts floor test; urges CM Vijay to continue Dravidian model welfare schemes
content: Opposition Leader Udhayanidhi Stalin asks the new government to continue the DMK government’s schemes

title: CRII condemns killing of Manipur tribal church leaders, urges judicial probe
content: CRII leaders say Rev. Sitlhou, former General Secretary of the Manipur Baptist Convention, was widely recognised for his lifelong commitment to justice and communal harmony

title: Andhra government sanctions 895 pensions for kidney, liver, heart transplant patients
content: MSME and SERP Minister stated that the decision was taken by Andhra CM Chandrababu Naidu to ensure that poor and eligible patients suffering from chronic illnesses do not face hardship

title: Four Assam Ministers allocated portfolios
content: The Governor approved the list of portfolios of the Ministers sent by the Chief Minister, officials said.

title: ECI announces schedule for Phase 3 of Special Intensive Revision of electoral rolls
content: ECI said the schedule for SIR Phase 3 has been set, taking into account the common field machinery used for the ongoing house listing under the Census exercise

title: COVID-19 linked to 22.1 million excess deaths globally between 2020 and 2023: WHO report
content: World Health Statistics report says the pandemic reversed a decade of gains in life expectancy and exposed deep vulnerabilities in global health systems, even as progress was recorded in HIV, sanitation, and disease control

title: ST quota: Why Congress and BJP are wary of raising their voice in Karnataka
content: Leadership tussle seems to have held back the Congress while the BJP is grappling with divided opinions and competing interests on the issue of ST quota

title: Cutting fuel use: Maharashtra CM Devendra Fadnavis rides motorcycle to Vidhan Bhavan
content: Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis arrived at the Vidhan Bhavan in South Mumbai on a motorbike from his residence 'Varsha'

title: Vijay’s government says it needs time to ‘restructure’ Magalir Urimai Thogai programme in Tamil Nadu
content: Monthly grant due for May will be credited into bank accounts of beneficiaries soon: State government

title: DMK president Stalin takes responsibility for party’s defeat in Tamil Nadu Assembly election
content: Besides drastic changes in party organisation, a field study is to be launched by Mr. Stalin as part of which the committee members will tour the State and submit a report within 20 days

title: Kerala Assembly polls | V. D. Satheesan: Captaining a resurgent Congress in Kerala
content: UDF’s emphatic win in the Kerala Assembly polls has not only silenced Satheesan’s critics, but reasserted the political supremacy of the Congress leader, who was given the task of reinvigorating the party and its disenchanted workers after he was chosen as the Leader of the Opposition following UDF’s electoral debacle in 2021

title: Woman gang-raped in sleeper bus in New Delhi
content: The two accused have been arrested in connection with the incident that took place on May 12, the sources said

title: Disparity between Rahul Gandhi's income, foreign trips' expenses, claims BJP
content: “According to the asset and liability declaration made by Rahul Gandhi in poll affidavits, the total disclosed assets stood at ₹55.38 lakh in 2004 and ₹20.39 crore in 2024,” Sambit Patra claimed

title: Mamata appears before Calcutta High Court to argue Bengal post-poll violence case; lawyers shout ‘thief’ slogans
content: According to the petitioner, several TMC leaders and workers were allegedly forced to flee their homes after the polls, while many of them were attacked “because of their association with the Trinamool Congress”

title: Suriya’s ’Karuppu’ shows cancelled; RJ Balaji apologises to fans, says he is ‘hopeful’ about evening release
content: Reports allege that the financial troubles of the film’s production banner, Dream Warrior Pictures, are the cause of the cancellation of the shows

title: Inspired by Vijay’s ‘Thamizhan’, Chennai man donates ₹22,674 to CM Relief Fund
content: Chennai-based Arun Loganathan, inspired by a Tamil movie starring Vijay, has contributed towards ‘fulfilling his individual debt responsibility’

title: Uniform policy: No new practices will be allowed, says Karnataka CM
content: Asked if the State Government will permit students to sport saffron shawls and turbans, Mr. Siddaramaiah said such shawls will not be allowed

title: Congress workers in Kerala welcome party leadership’s decision: KPCC president Sunny Joseph
content: Congress Legislature Party will meet at 4 p.m. on May 14 in Thiruvananthapuram and formally nominate V.D. Satheesan to the Chief Minister’s post

title: Chinese claims of ‘on site support’ to Pakistan during Operation Sindoor ‘corroborates’ what was known earlier: MEA
content: “Nations must reflect on whether supporting terror infrastructure affects their reputation and standing,” warns MEA; engineer confirms role of Chinese personnel in last year’s India-Pakistan clash

title: Two held for posting ‘offensive video’ targeting West Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari
content: Based on the complaints and initial verification, they were detained and subsequently arrested. Investigation is underway,” the officer said

title: Ten new MLCs sworn in at Maharashtra Vidhan Bhavan
content: Legislative Council chairperson Ram Shinde administered the oath to the members at the Central Hall in the Vidhan Bhavan

title: Red Fort bomb blast case: NIA files 7,500-page charge sheet against 10 accused
content: Apart from Dr. Nabi, the others named in the charge sheet are Aamir Rashid Mir, Jasir Bilal Wani, Dr. Muzamil Shakeel, Dr. Adeel Ahmed Rather, Dr. Shaheen Saeed, Mufti Irfan Ahmad Wagay, Soyab, Dr. Bilal Naseer Malla and Yasir Ahmad Dar

title: BRICS Foreign Ministers’ summit: Conflict in West Asia merits particular attention, says Jaishankar
content: BRICS Foreign Ministers and Heads of Delegation from Member and partner countries will participate in the meeting

title: West Bengal government directs schools to mandatorily include singing of Vande Mataram in morning assemblies
content: The directive states that every student must participate in singing the national song at the start of the school day, and heads of institutions have been instructed to ensure strict compliance

title: V.D. Satheesan to be next Chief Minister of Kerala as Congress leadership ends suspense
content: It was not immediately evident how the Congress leadership had eventually resolved the dispute among Mr. Satheesan, Ramesh Chennithala and and K.C. Venugopal, the three senior leaders who were in the running for the Chief Minister’s post

title: Congress demands Bandi Sanjay’s resignation
content: Congress calls for Bandi Sanjay’s resignation amid POCSO case involving his son, demanding accountability and full cooperation with the investigation

title: ST quota back at 3% raises concerns over  proportional representation
content: While the 1931 census put ST population at about 0.8%, with more tribes added to the list, it is now estimated to be over 7% of Karnataka’s population

title: What students in Karnataka can wear under the new uniform rules
content: While no student in Karnataka can be denied entry into an educational institution, classroom, examination hall or for any academic activity for wearing permitted symbols, in examinations, any national or State-level dress code rules already in place will continue to apply.

title: How is the anti-defection law going to operate in AIADMK’s case?
content: There are two grounds for disqualification in the event of defection — when a member voluntarily gives up membership of his or her political party and when a member votes or abstains from voting, contrary to any direction issued by his or her political party

title: ‘Limited’ religious symbols to be allowed with uniforms in Karnataka schools and colleges
content: The May 13 order revokes the previous BJP government’s February 5, 2022, directive, which had required students to wear prescribed uniforms and had become the basis for restrictions that later triggered the hijab row

title: Thoothukudi will see highest Tasmac closures of liquor shops near schools, places of worship
content: Employees working at Tasmac outlets in the Madurai region said the government must ensure adequate police deployment in these areas following the closures

title: Speeding Audi rams scooter in Bengaluru, kills man; wife, daughter hospitalised
content: a speeding Audi car rear-ended a gearless scooter near Chikkajala junction, killing the rider and injuring two others . the victim is Mahesh Kumar, 40, while his wife Kala M, 35, and their minor daughter are recovering in hospital .

title: Day After Trust Vote Win, Vijay Announces 2% Dearness Allowance Hike
content: The move is expected to benefit nearly 16 lakh government employees, teachers, pensioners, and family pensioners across the state. Despite the extra cost, the hike was given in view of their welfare, the government said.

title: 100 Killed As Heavy Rain, Storm Hit Uttar Pradesh; 21 Dead In Prayagraj
content: The weather changed suddenly last evening, with clouds of dust covering large parts of Uttar Pradesh.

title: Trump China Visit LIVE Updates: China, US Should Make Relationship Work And Never "Mess" It, Says Xi
content: Donald Trump's 2-Day China Visit Live Update: During the trip, US arms sales to Taiwan, China's controls on rare-earth exports, and the countries' raucous trade relationships are among the main issues expected to be taken up by Trump and Xi.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Rahul, Kharge, Priyanka: Inside The Kerala Power Contest That Forced Congress To Blink
content: Rahul lost in Kerala. Priyanka gained. Kharge is satisfied. Venugopal was checked. And Satheesan emerged as Chief Minister. That is the internal Congress story beneath the Kerala story.

title: Delhi High Court Hints At Contempt Action Against 'Some' In Liquor Policy Case
content: Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma was hearing the CBI's plea challenging the discharge of several accused, including Arvind Kejriwal and Manish Sisodia.

title: "Hatred": K Annamalai Slams DMK, TVK Over Sanatana Dharma Remarks
content: Chennai, May 14 (IANS) BJP leader K. Annamalai on Thursday launched a sharp attack on the DMK and the TVK over remarks made against Sanatana Dharma, accusing both parties of harbouring hatred towards Hindu beliefs and traditions.

title: Prateek Yadav Cremated In Lucknow, Family Bids Emotional Farewell
content: Lucknow, May 14 (IANS) The last rites of Prateek Yadav, the younger son of Samajwadi Party founder Mulayam Singh Yadav and husband of BJP leader Aparna Yadav, were performed at Baikunth Dham in Bhaisakund here on Thursday, with his father-in-law, vet

title: Video: "Chor" Slogans Raised As 'Lawyer' Mamata Banerjee Leaves Court
content: Trinamool leader and lawyer Kalyan Banerjee, who accompanied Mamata Banerjee to court, alleged that lawyers under the influence of the BJP attempted to heckle the former chief minister.

title: Xi Aims To Overcome 'Thucydides Trap' In Talks With Trump: What It Means
content: Xi has mentioned the "Thucydides Trap" before with regard to US-China relations, dating back to as early as 2014. The term refers to a theory that a rising power and an established hegemon are destined for war.

title: "No One In Control Of Anything": Court To Priya Kapur In Rs 30,000-Crore Case
content: Rani Kapur alleged that Priya Kapur is trying to take control of company finances before mediation by attempting to appoint two independent directors to RIPL which holds significant share of the family holdings.

title: 2 Academicians Go Missing In Balochistan, Search Efforts Underway
content: Authorities have launched efforts to trace the missing individuals, as concerns continue to grow over the safety of the senior university officials.

title: At Trump-Xi's Lavish Banquet, Huaiyang Cuisine To Set Tone For Diplomacy
content: For decades, China has used the powerful symbolism of food during landmark official events and when receiving distinguished foreign visitors.

title: Kerala CM Announcement LIVE Updates: "God-Given Responsibility": VD Satheesan Thanks Congress For Selecting Him As Chief Minister
content: Kerala Chief Minister Live Updates: A formal announcement is expected today, once Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge returns to Delhi and holds further talks with senior figures, including Sonia Gandhi.

title: Who Is Golshifteh Farahani? Actor Linked To Emmanuel Macron In 'Slap' Row
content: Paris Match political correspondent Florian Tardif suggested the incident followed a jealous argument after Brigitte Macron saw messages exchanged between Emmanuel Macron and Farahani.

title: India Calls For UN Reforms, Gaza Ceasefire At BRICS Foreign Ministers' Meet
content: Jaishankar opened his address with a frank assessment of a deteriorating global landscape. Conflicts, climate events, and the Covid pandemic, he said, had been "key contributory factors" to widening vulnerabilities.

title: China's Door Will Open Wider To US Businesses: Xi Jinping Tells US CEOs
content: Trump-Xi Summit: The US President wants China to stop weaponising supply chains, especially rare earths that power US tech and defence industries.
